entitySuffixRule: Must contain the words "limited liability company" or "limited company," or the abbreviations "L.L.C.," "LLC," "L.C.," or "LC." The word "Limited" may be abbreviated "Ltd." and "Company" may be abbreviated "Co."
distinguishabilityNote: Must be distinguishable in the records of the Secretary of State from the name of each person that is not an individual and is incorporated, organized, or authorized to transact business in Vermont. A name that is not distinguishable may be used if the other holder consents in a signed record and undertakes to change its noncomplying name, or if the applicant delivers a certified copy of a final judgment establishing its right to use the name.
nameReservationCostBasis: The Secretary of State fee to reserve a name is $25.
notes: Vermont's LLC Act (11 V.S.A. ch. 25) uses the standard distinguishable-on-the-records test and clears a conflict only with the other holder's consent-plus-undertaking or a certified final judgment. There is no banned-word list. A reservation lasts 120 days for $25. The LLC formation fee is $155 and the annual report ($45) is due within three months after the LLC's fiscal year end.
